news_summary: Today's news highlights global efforts to amplify diverse voices and promote sustainable, fair, and safe e-commerce practices through international collaboration.
news_summary_fr: L'actualité d'aujourd'hui met en avant les efforts déployés à l'échelle mondiale pour faire entendre des voix diverses et promouvoir des pratiques de commerce électronique durables, équitables et sûres grâce à la collaboration internationale.
news_summary_es: Las noticias de hoy destacan los esfuerzos a nivel mundial para dar mayor visibilidad a voces diversas y promover prácticas de comercio electrónico sostenibles, justas y seguras mediante la colaboración internacional.

articles.title: Announcing Global Voices Spotlight!
articles.summary: We are launching a new approach to our mission of amplifying the voices of those underrepresented in the digital sphere: Global Voices Spotlight.
articles.content:
As the need for responsible, multi-perspective news coverage grows increasingly urgent, and the situation for media organizations and news providers is made increasingly difficult, Global Voices is responding by leaning into our strengths: our twenty years of building a worldwide community and centering the stories our contributors, translators, researchers, and fellows feel are important. Stellar examples of this include the Climate Justice Fellowship, where we paired independent Sinophone and other global majority country journalists to assess the role of China in mitigating the global climate crisis; collaborative articles like this one on flooding events around the world; and cross-border stories like this one tracing human trafficking across the borders of South Asia and China.

To fully leverage our amazing network and enable our readers to explore the full breadth of our cross-border, multilingual work, we are launching a new approach to our mission of amplifying the voices of those underrepresented in the digital sphere: Global Voices Spotlight.

In addition to our regular coverage, every month we will be choosing an urgent theme to explore in depth across all our regions. This range of perspectives will be grounded in our Global Voices values of human and digital rights — as always. It will also be complemented by collaborative articles, online panels, and analysis of key narratives using our Civic Media Observatory methodology. Cross-cutting essays will highlight the trends, exceptions, and insights that we gain from exploring the vital issues of today with contributors from across the globe, instead of the narrow reporting that focuses on the rich, famous, and available for comment.

With these special, thematic issues, we will bring our readers new understanding, raise the profile of important topics, and illuminate connections across regions, borders, and languages. We’re confident that, in every issue, you’ll find more than one angle that you hadn’t considered before.

We’re excited to start this process with a collection of articles on how the use of, promotion of, and resistance to AI is playing out for the Global Majority, from the way AI is weaponized in elections and conflicts, to the consequences for low-resource languages, to the impacts on human rights, and much more.

In May, we’ll be looking at climate solutions around the world, from technology to society, and with all the complications and nuance that you expect from Global Voices stories. We’ll be announcing more themes soon, and would love reader feedback on what you would like us to explore.

We’d also love to do more on each of these topics than we can afford to do with our current funding levels. On our donation page, you’ll find opportunities to support specific themes. Contribute to the issues on topics you care about, and help us add more articles, analysis, and multimedia initiatives, for more cross-cutting insights to help you understand the world.
articles.reasoning: The article announces a new initiative by Global Voices to amplify underrepresented voices globally, focusing on urgent themes like climate justice, AI impacts, and human rights. This approach promotes diverse, cross-border perspectives and aims to raise awareness on significant societal issues, thereby having a meaningful positive impact on public understanding and digital rights advocacy.
articles.personality_title: Global Voices launches monthly Spotlight on urgent global issues
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Global Voices is a group that shares news and stories from people around the world who are often not heard in big media. They have worked for 20 years to connect journalists, translators, and researchers from many countries.

**What happened** – Global Voices announced a new project called Global Voices Spotlight. Every month, they will focus on one important theme and share stories from different regions and languages. Their first theme looks at how artificial intelligence (AI) affects people worldwide, including its role in elections and human rights. In May, they will explore climate solutions from around the world.

**Impact** – This new approach helps readers understand big issues from many points of view, not just the usual news sources. It connects stories across borders and languages, showing how problems and solutions affect different communities. It also highlights voices that are often ignored, helping people learn about important topics like digital rights and social justice.

**What's next step** – Global Voices will keep choosing new themes every month and invite readers to suggest topics. They also hope to raise money to create even more articles and multimedia projects on these themes, making the stories richer and more detailed.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Global Voices Spotlight brings diverse, global stories on urgent issues to readers each month, helping everyone understand complex topics from new angles.
articles.personality_title_fr: Global Voices lance Spotlight mensuel sur les enjeux mondiaux urgents
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Global Voices est un groupe qui partage des nouvelles et des histoires de personnes souvent peu entendues dans les grands médias. Depuis 20 ans, ils relient des journalistes, traducteurs et chercheurs de nombreux pays.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Global Voices a annoncé un nouveau projet appelé Global Voices Spotlight. Chaque mois, ils se concentreront sur un thème important et partageront des histoires de différentes régions et langues. Leur premier thème porte sur l’impact de l’intelligence artificielle (IA) dans le monde, y compris son rôle dans les élections et les droits humains. En mai, ils exploreront les solutions climatiques à travers le monde.

**Impact** – Cette nouvelle approche aide les lecteurs à comprendre de grands enjeux sous plusieurs points de vue, pas seulement ceux des médias habituels. Elle relie les histoires au-delà des frontières et des langues, montrant comment les problèmes et solutions touchent différentes communautés. Elle met aussi en lumière des voix souvent ignorées, aidant à mieux connaître des sujets comme les droits numériques et la justice sociale.

**Prochaines étapes** – Global Voices continuera de choisir de nouveaux thèmes chaque mois et invitera les lecteurs à proposer des sujets. Ils espèrent aussi collecter des fonds pour créer encore plus d’articles et de projets multimédias sur ces thèmes, rendant les histoires plus riches et détaillées.

**Message clé en une phrase** – Global Voices Spotlight offre chaque mois des histoires mondiales diverses sur des enjeux urgents, aidant chacun à comprendre des sujets complexes sous de nouveaux angles.
articles.personality_title_es: Global Voices lanza Spotlight mensual sobre temas globales urgentes
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Global Voices es un grupo que comparte noticias e historias de personas que a menudo no son escuchadas en los grandes medios. Han trabajado 20 años conectando periodistas, traductores e investigadores de muchos países.

**Qué pasó** – Global Voices anunció un nuevo proyecto llamado Global Voices Spotlight. Cada mes se centrarán en un tema importante y compartirán historias de diferentes regiones y idiomas. Su primer tema trata sobre cómo la inteligencia artificial (IA) afecta a las personas en todo el mundo, incluyendo su papel en elecciones y derechos humanos. En mayo explorarán soluciones climáticas de todo el mundo.

**Impacto** – Este nuevo enfoque ayuda a los lectores a entender grandes problemas desde muchos puntos de vista, no solo las fuentes habituales de noticias. Conecta historias a través de fronteras e idiomas, mostrando cómo los problemas y soluciones afectan a diferentes comunidades. También destaca voces que a menudo son ignoradas, ayudando a las personas a aprender sobre temas importantes como los derechos digitales y la justicia social.

**Próximos pasos** – Global Voices seguirá eligiendo nuevos temas cada mes e invitará a los lectores a sugerir temas. También esperan recaudar fondos para crear más artículos y proyectos multimedia sobre estos temas, haciendo las historias más ricas y detalladas.

**Resumen en una frase** – Global Voices Spotlight ofrece cada mes historias globales diversas sobre temas urgentes, ayudando a todos a entender asuntos complejos desde nuevas perspectivas.
articles.image_prompt: A warm, detailed painting of a vibrant globe surrounded by interconnected hands of diverse colors gently holding symbolic elements like a glowing network of threads representing multilingual communication, stylized waves symbolizing climate and flooding, and subtle abstract shapes evoking digital connections and AI, all set against a soft, natural color palette that conveys unity, collaboration, and cross-border storytelling.

articles.title: Press release - MEPs conclude China visit advocating sustainable e-commerce, fair competition and product safety
articles.summary: The Internal Market and Consumer Protection committee delegation was in Beijing and Shanghai from 31 March to 2 April.Committee on the Internal Market and Consumer Protection Source : © European Union, 2026 - EP
articles.content:
MEPs addressed current e-commerce challenges and the profound impact that they are having on the European single market.

During their first official visit for eight years, MEPs met with Chinese parliamentarians and regulators to study the local business model and its market supervision framework. They looked into the differences between the European and Chinese consumer protection systems, and possible areas for collaboration. They visited dominant e-commerce platforms Alibaba, Shein and Temu, EU company representatives and Chinese customs authorities.

The discussions focused on the massive surge in e-commerce parcels arriving in Europe from China (representing 91% of all small parcels), the increasingly uneven playing field between the two parties. The structural overcapacities in Chinese production models are leading to the flooding of the internal market, harming fair competition, MEPs warned. In light of recent scandals involving Chinese e-commerce platforms, the MEPs expressed their particular concerns about the systemic health and safety risks for European consumers and the lack of platform oversight. In this context, they urged the platforms’ representatives to put the necessary preventative measures in place and to abide by the EU’s digital and consumer safety requirements. The MEPs stressed that safety and digital standards are non-negotiable and apply to all companies equally.

Quote

Concluding the visit on Thursday, the chair of the Internal Market and Consumer Protection Committee and delegation head Anna Cavazzini (Greens/EFA, Germany) said:

“It was important to come to China to meet with our counterparts and representatives of key e-commerce companies, to explain our rules in person. We have seen the e-commerce model shifting dramatically in recent years- it is a new reality but not a sustainable one if the rules are not enforced. While all consumers should have access to affordable consumer products, they should not have to worry about their health and safety when shopping.

More needs to be done to ensure companies are held accountable when rules are not followed. Whether it is about selling consumer products, removing dangerous toys from the market before they reach children, or paying customs fees – the same rules apply to everyone. We want to see rapid improvements and we want to ensure that our warnings are being taken seriously by China’s authorities and companies”.

Composition of the delegation

The MEPs that took part in the delegation were IMCO chair and delegation head Anna Cavazzini (Greens/EFA, DE), Andreas Schwab (EPP, DE), Dirk Gotink (EPP, NL), Christel Schaldemose (S&D, DK), Pierre Jouvet (S&D, FR), Virginie Joron (Pfe, FR), Piotr Muller (ECR, PL), Stephanie Yon-Courtin (Renew, FR), and Engin Eroglu (Renew, DE), chair of Parliament’s delegation to China.
articles.reasoning: The article reports on a significant diplomatic and regulatory effort by European MEPs to promote sustainable e-commerce, fair competition, and consumer product safety in the context of EU-China trade relations. This has broad positive implications for consumer health and safety across Europe, addressing systemic risks and advocating for accountability and enforcement of safety standards. The visit and discussions represent meaningful progress toward protecting consumers and ensuring fair market conditions, with clear context and details provided.
articles.personality_title: MEPs visit China to promote safer and fairer e-commerce between Europe and China
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – European Members of Parliament (MEPs) from the Internal Market and Consumer Protection committee visited China for the first time in eight years to discuss e-commerce challenges and consumer safety.

**What happened** – From March 31 to April 2, MEPs met with Chinese parliamentarians, regulators, and representatives from major e-commerce platforms like Alibaba, Shein, and Temu. They studied China’s business models and consumer protection systems, while sharing Europe’s rules and concerns. The delegation also spoke with EU company representatives and Chinese customs officials.

**Impact** – The visit highlighted that 91% of small parcels arriving in Europe come from China, creating an uneven market. MEPs warned that too many products from China flood the European market, harming fair competition. They also raised concerns about health and safety risks from some Chinese e-commerce platforms and stressed that all companies must follow EU safety and digital rules equally.

**What's next step** – MEPs want faster improvements in how Chinese companies follow EU rules. They expect Chinese authorities and businesses to take their warnings seriously and work on stronger controls to protect European consumers.

**One-sentence takeaway** – European lawmakers are working directly with China to ensure safer products and fair competition in online shopping between the two regions.
articles.personality_title_fr: Les députés européens en Chine pour promouvoir un e-commerce plus sûr et équitable
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Des députés européens du comité du marché intérieur et de la protection des consommateurs ont visité la Chine pour la première fois en huit ans afin de discuter des défis du commerce en ligne et de la sécurité des consommateurs.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – Du 31 mars au 2 avril, les députés ont rencontré des parlementaires chinois, des régulateurs et des représentants de grandes plateformes comme Alibaba, Shein et Temu. Ils ont étudié les modèles commerciaux chinois et les systèmes de protection des consommateurs, tout en partageant les règles et préoccupations européennes. La délégation a aussi échangé avec des représentants européens et les autorités douanières chinoises.

**Impact** – La visite a montré que 91 % des petits colis arrivant en Europe viennent de Chine, créant un marché déséquilibré. Les députés ont averti que trop de produits chinois inondent le marché européen, nuisant à la concurrence loyale. Ils ont aussi exprimé des inquiétudes sur les risques pour la santé liés à certaines plateformes et ont insisté pour que toutes les entreprises respectent les règles de sécurité et numériques de l’UE.

**Prochaine étape** – Les députés veulent que les entreprises chinoises respectent plus rapidement les règles européennes. Ils attendent des autorités chinoises qu’elles prennent leurs avertissements au sérieux et renforcent les contrôles pour protéger les consommateurs européens.

**Résumé en une phrase** – Les législateurs européens collaborent directement avec la Chine pour garantir des produits plus sûrs et une concurrence équitable dans le commerce en ligne entre les deux régions.
articles.personality_title_es: Eurodiputados visitan China para promover un comercio electrónico más seguro y justo
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Miembros del Parlamento Europeo (MEPs) del comité de Mercado Interior y Protección del Consumidor visitaron China por primera vez en ocho años para hablar sobre los retos del comercio electrónico y la seguridad del consumidor.

**Qué pasó** – Del 31 de marzo al 2 de abril, los eurodiputados se reunieron con parlamentarios chinos, reguladores y representantes de grandes plataformas como Alibaba, Shein y Temu. Estudiaron los modelos comerciales chinos y sus sistemas de protección al consumidor, mientras compartían las normas y preocupaciones europeas. También hablaron con representantes de empresas europeas y autoridades aduaneras chinas.

**Impacto** – La visita mostró que el 91 % de los pequeños paquetes que llegan a Europa provienen de China, creando un mercado desigual. Los eurodiputados advirtieron que demasiados productos chinos inundan el mercado europeo, afectando la competencia justa. También expresaron preocupación por riesgos para la salud y seguridad en algunas plataformas y destacaron que todas las empresas deben cumplir las reglas de seguridad y digitales de la UE.

**Próximo paso** – Los eurodiputados quieren que las empresas chinas cumplan las normas europeas más rápido. Esperan que las autoridades y empresas chinas tomen en serio sus advertencias y refuercen los controles para proteger a los consumidores europeos.

**Resumen en una frase** – Los legisladores europeos trabajan directamente con China para asegurar productos más seguros y competencia justa en las compras en línea entre ambas regiones.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting showing two stylized, abstract hands—one adorned with European stars and the other with Chinese motifs—gently exchanging a glowing parcel wrapped in a digital network pattern, set against a balanced backdrop of simplified e-commerce platform icons and interconnected safety shields symbolizing fair competition and consumer protection.
